title: Is there a release date for Evangelion 30
display_title: Is there a release date for Evangelion 30
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion 3.0 to be released...? Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Sep 21, 2009 8:26 am According to a recent Newtype interview, as discussed on tokyo3cn , reposted to the Evangelion ML , and once more reposted here for your delight and consumption, Rebuild of Evangelion 3.0 is due out in the Summer of 2010. This is earlier than expected, as it took nearly two years between 1.0 and 2.0. Apparently, the films have been so successful that they’re not limiting the budget on 3.0, and with the staff from the previous two films, they’ll be adding new staff, maintaining the same level of quality, and pushing the film out much earlier than expected. The only downside is that the directors/supervisiors won’t be able to do interviews/press conferences. I’d say that’s a pretty fair trade, wouldn’t you?

[]: Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Mon Sep 21, 2009 8:54 am o rly? this seems from quite a long time ago, but it's the first time i heard of it anywhere. or has it already been spammed multiple times in the spoilerific threads? would be nice to have the actual article and not some dude on the internet

[]: Post by Captain_Morgan » Mon Sep 21, 2009 9:10 am If this is true, oh sweetness. Shouldn't Evangelion: Final be released next summer, as well?

[]: Post by trueno_twentyone » Mon Sep 21, 2009 9:35 am That's sweet! 3.0 by summer of 2010. Not bad. Same as the elections here in the Philippines. Lots of things to expect this summer, I'm getting excited! @captain : By the looks of it, it would be two different movies. With its success, they'll be milking it more.

[]: Post by R-U-4-Zeruel » Mon Sep 21, 2009 10:17 am I can't wait ! 3.0 is going to kick to much arse, can't wait to see elevnth and twelve angel (hopefully lilin is not one of them) two more angel fights please

[]: Post by esselfortium » Mon Sep 21, 2009 10:47 am Awesome! If 3.0 and Final aren't planned to be released together anymore, I also have to wonder if they're still going to be shorter than 1.0 and 2.0...

[]: Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Sep 21, 2009 11:08 am TMBounty_Hunter wrote: this seems from quite a long time ago

[]: Post by Legendary » Mon Sep 21, 2009 11:08 am About time. 1.0 has been out for like a decade or something. :P

[]: Post by SaltyJoe » Mon Sep 21, 2009 12:41 pm OMG, holy crap, and other stuff like that! Captain_Morgan wrote: Shouldn't Evangelion: Final be released next summer, as well?

[]: Post by Axell » Mon Sep 21, 2009 1:00 pm This will keep me happy till it gets postponed

[]: Post by Heromaster111 » Mon Sep 21, 2009 2:47 pm Axell wrote: This will keep me happy till it gets postponed

[]: Post by Electric Sachiel » Mon Sep 21, 2009 3:01 pm i can only hope that this article brings us info thats accurate and will actually come to fruition. How many times in the past have we had announcements like these for films like 1.0 and 2.0 only to see their release dates pushed back significantly? And i would also hope that with an increased budget and enrollment of staff to help, the movie's screening time will be extended. 3.0.....please be longer than 45 min. I could care less about 4.0 right now. Anno and the creative/production team at Khara should seriously consider taking more time to tell the story in 3.0, instead of rushing to close to bring in 4.0

[]: Post by TMSIDR » Mon Sep 21, 2009 3:09 pm That's really strange because Hidenori Matsubara told on the convention Connichi 2009 3 days ago, that only the preparations started for the 3rd movie, so a release in less a year seems to be unlikely when we take a look at the gap between 1.0 and 2.0. Perhaps money can accelarate the production but I think it still will take longer. It's funny that they mentioned that there won't be interviews because Anno didn't do any interviews since before the release of 1.0 (the exception was in the "artbook" of 1.0).

[]: Post by NAveryW » Mon Sep 21, 2009 3:10 pm Onegeek wrote: Interviewer: So you're saing that Q is complete? Producer: Yes. The draw are just completed. Its publication will be in summer 2010.

[]: Post by Great Genius Shinji-Sama » Mon Sep 21, 2009 4:15 pm So Final and Q still come out together right? I mean we'll get both from Japan next Summer correct? Because I will so download it if thats right!

[]: Post by AchtungAffen » Mon Sep 21, 2009 4:18 pm I read the original Japanese post on Tokyo3cn (yes, they did a Japanese, not chinese, transcription), and it doesn't say shit about budget and stuff. What it said was that after Jyo, the staff worked on both Ha and Q at the same time, or mostly parallel. The translation made by the guy who posted on the EvaML is plain wrong.

[]: Post by NAveryW » Mon Sep 21, 2009 4:46 pm At least that means they knew where they were going when they made 2.0 . Hooray! Also, I doubt there's going to be a 4.0 at this point. It was initially planned to come out along with 3.0 , and speculation arose later that it may become a separate movie. Now that there is no 3.0 anymore, just Q , I'm going to assume that's the final movie until confirmation otherwise.

[]: Post by Joseph the PRPD » Mon Sep 21, 2009 5:26 pm I hope it actually happens. Heromaster111 wrote: Axell wrote: This will keep me happy till it gets postponed

[]: Post by carla » Mon Sep 21, 2009 5:33 pm regardless of if it's true or if the translation's accurate, i'm glad there's buzz, at least. it might work like pulling on anno's ear, who knows besides that, bigger budget = ME LIKEY i hope at least that part is true.
